task_completion.description: Navigate to WooCommerce Accounts & Privacy settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the WordPress admin dashboard with the WooCommerce > Settings > Accounts & Privacy tab open.

task_completion.description: Enable the setting to remove personal data from orders on request
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox for 'Remove personal data from orders on request' is checked, and the agent clicks 'Save changes' or a 'Settings saved' confirmation is visible.

task_completion.description: Add a data erasure request for the target customer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Tools > Erase Personal Data screen is visible, and the email 'sarah.connor.privacy@example.com' is entered into the 'Add Data Erasure Request' form or appears in the pending requests list.

task_completion.description: Execute the data erasure request to anonymize order data
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks 'Force Erasure' or 'Erase Personal Data' for the request associated with 'sarah.connor.privacy@example.com', or the request list shows the status as 'Completed'.

task_completion.description: Delete the user account for the target customer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Users > All Users screen is visible, and the agent deletes the user 'Sarah Connor' (e.g., clicking 'Delete', confirming deletion, or a 'User deleted' confirmation message is shown).

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress admin GUI to configure WooCommerce settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show navigation through the WordPress admin menus (WooCommerce > Settings) rather than terminal commands like 'wp option update'.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress admin GUI to process the erasure request and delete the user
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Tools > Erase Personal Data and Users pages, not terminal commands like 'wp user delete'.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the application without using browser developer tools to fake success messages
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are open to manipulate the DOM for fake confirmation messages.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Customer: Sarah Connor. Target Email: sarah.connor.privacy@example.com. The WooCommerce setting to enable is 'woocommerce_erasure_request_removes_order_data' (labeled 'Remove personal data from orders on request' in the GUI under WooCommerce > Settings > Accounts & Privacy). The expected value for this setting is 'yes' (checked).
